mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that reduces discount rates for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in payrolls (PAYEMS) and declining unemployment (UNRATE) signals a tightening labor market, which drives corporate earnings growth and positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day returns are negative in 60%+ of instances where PAYEMS > 100k and UNRATE declines > 0.2pp.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly GDP contraction (GDPC1) signals economic weakness that triggers central bank intervention, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as risk-on sentiment returns.
failure_condition: 60-day returns are negative in 70%+ of instances following GDPC1 declines > 1% YoY.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects stronger manufacturing activity, which boosts corporate profits and generates positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day returns are negative in 50%+ of instances where INDPRO rises > 1% MoM.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 2.5% (CPIAUCSL) forces central banks to raise rates, creating headwinds for equity valuations and negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day returns are positive in 60%+ of instances when CPIAUCSL exceeds 2.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A surge in housing starts (HOUST) combined with rising consumer spending (RSAFS) indicates strong demand, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days during expansionary regimes.
failure_condition: 5-day returns are negative in 50%+ of instances where both HOUST and RSAFS exceed thresholds.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate below 5% signals a tight labor market that pressures wage growth, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days due to inflationary risks.
failure_condition: 60-day returns are positive in 60%+ of instances when UNRATE remains below 5%.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in industrial production (INDPRO) during rising oil prices (CPIAUCSL) signals stagflationary pressures, generating negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day returns are positive in 50%+ of instances where both INDPRO falls below 105 and CPIAUCSL exceeds 2.5%.
